site.title: Claude Code | Developer Fundamentals
site.description: A comprehensive beginner's guide to Claude Code — learn agentic workflows, MCP integrations, and terminal-native AI development from scratch.
site.og_description: Master Claude Code: agentic loops, sub-agents, skills, MCP servers, and polyglot orchestration.
hero.subtitle: Enter the Agentic Era with Claude Code. Move beyond basic IDE autocomplete to an autonomous assistant that reads, plans, and executes directly in your terminal.
hero.cta_primary: Initialize Workspace <svg class="ml-2 w-4 h-4" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="2" d="M14 5l7 7m0 0l-7 7m7-7H3"></path></svg>
hero.terminal_cmd: $ claude "refactor the auth middleware to use JWT instead of sessions"
hero.terminal_line2: Thinking: I need to install jsonwebtoken and update the guards.
loop.title: Beyond Autocomplete: The Agentic Loop
loop.desc: Unlike standard AI coding tools that wait for you to highlight code, Claude Code thinks before it acts. It operates autonomously through a three-phase continuous loop.
loop.step1_desc: The agent navigates your directory using native Bash commands. It reads relevant files, parses your package.json, and builds a contextual map of your architecture.
loop.step2_desc: Using features like Extended Thinking (Ultrathink), Claude pauses to draft a strategy before modifying code. This critical step drastically reduces AI-generated technical debt.
loop.step3_desc: It edits files, runs terminal commands (like compiling), and verifies its own work by running your test suite. If tests fail, it loops back to step 1 to debug.
loop.link: Dive deeper into the Agentic Loop
collaboration.title: Ecosystem: Agents, Skills & Collaboration
collaboration.desc: Claude Code is not a single monolith. It is an ecosystem where a Lead Agent orchestrates specialized Subagents and dynamically loads Skills to solve complex engineering problems.
collaboration.agent_desc: The Lead Agent is the brain. It maintains the overarching context of your prompt, decides which files to read, and delegates bounded tasks to specialized Subagents (like an exploration agent or a testing agent).
collaboration.skill_desc: A Skill is a prompt-based meta-tool. Instead of hardcoded plugins, a Skill is just a directory with markdown files (e.g., SKILL.md). By using Progressive Disclosure, Claude dynamically alters its own system prompt and tool permissions based on the skill loaded.
collaboration.collab_desc: The true power is in orchestration. The Lead Agent can load the golang-pro Skill to understand Go idioms, utilize an MCP Server to securely fetch database schemas, and then spin up a Subagent to write concurrent tests.
collaboration.link: Read the API Docs on Agent Skills
claudemd.title: Context is Everything: The CLAUDE.md File
claudemd.desc1: The biggest pitfall of AI coding is accumulating technical debt. Prevent this by treating the AI like a new hire: provide an onboarding document.
claudemd.desc2: Placing a CLAUDE.md file in your project root sets persistent boundaries. Claude reads this automatically on startup to understand your architectural rules, formatting preferences, and test mandates.
claudemd.check1: Enforce Clean Architecture or MVC patterns.
claudemd.check2: Mandate that tests must be run after every edit.
claudemd.check3: Protect specific directories (e.g., "Never modify DB migrations").
claudemd.link: Learn more about Best Practices & Project Conventions
architecture.label: Reference Architecture
architecture.title: Anatomy of an AI Coding Agent
architecture.desc: A modern AI coding agent is more than a language model. It is a layered system that reads context, plans actions, routes to specialized tools, and delivers verified output.
workflow.label: Lifecycle
workflow.title: The Agentic Workflow Lifecycle
workflow.desc: From initial prompt to committed code, an AI coding agent follows a structured lifecycle with iterative loops, external tool integration, and human checkpoints.
setup.title: Initialize Your First Workspace From Scratch
setup.desc: Follow these precise steps to properly configure your environment. Setting up correctly prevents common PATH and execution errors.
setup.step1_desc:
Ensure Node.js (npm) is installed.

Windows Users: Claude Code requires WSL2 (Windows Subsystem for Linux). Do not run it in standard Command Prompt. For best performance, keep your project files in the WSL filesystem (~/), not mounted Windows drives (/mnt/c/).
setup.step2_desc: Install Claude Code globally via npm to make it accessible anywhere in your terminal.
setup.step3_desc: Link your terminal to your Anthropic Console. Running this command opens a secure browser window for OAuth validation.
setup.step4_desc: Navigate to your project root. Typing claude drops you into the interactive agent interface.
extend.title: Extend Claude: Build & Setup MCPs and Skills
extend.desc: Give your agent access to the real world. Learn how to configure official MCP servers, build custom Skills from scratch, and orchestrate them dynamically.
extend.mcp_desc1: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers connect Claude to external APIs, databases, and tools. Official MCPs like Firecrawl (for web scraping) or GitHub can be added instantly.
extend.mcp_desc2: How to add newly: Create a .mcp.json file in your project root, or use the CLI command claude mcp add.
extend.skill_desc1: A Skill is a prompt-based meta-tool that teaches Claude specific workflows or standards (e.g., golang-pro). They act as progressive disclosure, injecting context only when needed.
extend.skill_desc2: How to build & add: Create a directory inside .claude/skills/. Inside, create a SKILL.md file with YAML frontmatter. Invoke it via /skill load my-skill.
extend.link: Read the official guide on Extending Claude Code
advanced.title: Advanced: Hooks, Permissions, Slash Commands & Worktrees
advanced.desc: Once you're comfortable with the basics, these features unlock the real productivity gains. This is where Claude Code stops being a chatbot and starts being a system integrator.
advanced.hooks_desc: Shell commands the harness runs on lifecycle events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, SessionStart). Perfect for auto-formatting, lint gates, or notifications — because hooks run in your shell, not Claude.
advanced.permissions_desc: Four modes control how much autonomy Claude gets. Start with default, use plan for exploratory work, and only opt into bypass inside sandboxes or throwaway containers.
advanced.commands_desc: Drop a markdown file into .claude/commands/ and it becomes a reusable slash command. Arguments via $ARGUMENTS. Great for repetitive flows: code review, release notes, bug triage.
advanced.subagents_desc: A subagent is a specialized agent with its own tools, system prompt, and (optionally) its own git worktree. Use them to parallelize independent work or to keep focused searches out of your main context.
advanced.planvsthink_desc: These are frequently confused. They solve different problems:
advanced.planvsthink_rule: Rule of thumb: Plan mode for risky refactors you want to review. Extended thinking for hard problems the model keeps getting wrong.
advanced.worktrees_desc: Run agents in parallel on separate branches without touching your working tree. Each agent gets an isolated checkout — no context collision, no merge chaos.
advanced.memory_desc: Claude reads memory files in a specific order. Later files override earlier ones. You can also pull in shared rules with @path/to/file imports.
advanced.memory_project: Architecture rules, test mandates, team conventions (checked in)
advanced.memory_user: Personal preferences across every project (not checked in)
advanced.memory_imports: Pull in shared rule files from anywhere in the repo
advanced.debug_desc: Run claude --debug for verbose logs, or /doctor inside a session to diagnose install/auth issues.
advanced.cost_desc: Use /cost to see token spend. Route routine tasks through Haiku or Sonnet; save Opus for hard reasoning.
advanced.bg_desc: Long-running commands (builds, test suites) can run in the background. Claude is notified on completion — you're not blocked waiting.
orchestrator.title: Orchestrating Polyglot Microservices
orchestrator.desc: Working in a complex repository? Learn how to configure Claude Code as a Lead Agent to dispatch tasks across iOS, Android, Go, and .NET pipelines.
orchestrator.step1_desc: Create a master CLAUDE.md at the root of your project. This acts as the "router." Instruct Claude to identify which microservice needs changing and to load the appropriate domain-specific skill before writing code.
orchestrator.step2_desc: Equip the agent with the right tools for each environment using MCP and local skills:
orchestrator.platform_android_desc: Load robust skills for MVVM + Jetpack Compose.
orchestrator.step3_desc: Prompt Claude sequentially to act as a full-stack orchestrator:
orchestrator.step3_example: "We are adding a user avatar feature. 1. Update the .NET backend API. 2. Update the Go image processing worker. 3. Implement the UI in both iOS and Android clients using their specific skills."
orchestrator.link: Learn about Common Workflows & Subagent Orchestration
tips.title: Pro Tips for Beginners
tips.desc: Avoid common pitfalls and technical debt. Here are the golden rules to ensure a smooth transition into agentic development.
tips.tip1_desc: Because Claude Code modifies files directly, always commit your current git state before giving it a complex task. If the agent misunderstands and breaks something, a quick git reset --hard is your ultimate safety net.
tips.tip2_desc: AI gets confused in long, sprawling conversations. Use the /clear or /compact commands frequently to reset the agent's memory. This keeps costs down and ensures the agent stays laser-focused on the immediate task.
tips.tip3_desc:
Don't ask "Build me a login system." Instead, orchestrate the process:
1. "Explore the auth folder."
2. "Draft a plan for a JWT login system."
3. "Execute step 1 of the plan."
tips.tip4_desc: Claude has its own documentation built-in. If you don't know how to do something, just type /help or ask the agent directly: "How do I add an MCP server for PostgreSQL?"
tips.link: Read the comprehensive Best Practices guide
security.title: Enterprise-Grade Security by Default
security.desc: Giving an AI access to your terminal requires trust. Claude Code is built with stringent safety mechanisms to ensure you are always in control.
security.sandbox_desc: Executes bash scripts in isolated environments. This architecture achieves an 84% reduction in manual permission prompts without compromising host machine security.
security.human_desc: Claude will automatically pause and explicitly request approval before executing destructive commands, installing third-party dependencies, or making outward network requests.
security.data_desc: Designed for enterprise compliance (SOC 2, ISO 27001). Your proprietary enterprise code and API keys are strictly protected and never used to train future Anthropic models.
comparison.label: Landscape
comparison.title: AI Coding Tools Compared
comparison.desc: A side-by-side comparison of popular AI coding assistants. GitHub stats are fetched live; feature flags are curated manually.
comparison.note: GitHub stats fetched live. Features curated manually.
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.desc: Common questions and troubleshooting tips for getting started with Claude Code.
faq.q1: Why does claude command not found after install?
faq.a1: Your npm global bin directory may not be in your PATH. Run npm config get prefix and add the resulting bin/ directory to your shell profile (~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc).
faq.q2: Claude keeps asking for permission on every command. How do I reduce prompts?
faq.a2: Use claude config to adjust permission settings. You can allow specific tools (like Bash, Read, Write) to run without prompting. The --dangerously-skip-permissions flag exists but is not recommended for general use.
faq.q3: What is the difference between CLAUDE.md and .claude/settings.json?
faq.a3: CLAUDE.md provides natural-language context and instructions (architecture rules, coding standards). .claude/settings.json is for machine configuration: MCP servers, permission rules, and environment variables.
faq.q4: Can I use Claude Code with VS Code or JetBrains?
faq.a4: Yes. Claude Code has official extensions for both VS Code and JetBrains IDEs. Install the extension from the marketplace, and it connects to the same CLI under the hood. You can also use it as a standalone desktop app or via the web at claude.ai/code.
faq.q5: Does Claude Code work on Windows without WSL?
faq.a5: No. Claude Code requires a Unix-like environment. On Windows, you must use WSL2. For best performance, keep your project files inside the WSL filesystem (~/) rather than on mounted Windows drives (/mnt/c/).
faq.q6: How do I keep costs down?
faq.a6: Use /compact frequently to compress conversation history. Use /clear between unrelated tasks. Break large tasks into focused steps instead of one massive prompt. Toggle /fast mode for routine tasks that don't need extended thinking.
footer.cta_desc: Start orchestrating your codebase today. Available for macOS, Linux, and Windows (via WSL).
